raw_title: Shaping Sustainable Communication on Instagram: How Digital Influencers in Portugal Engage Consumers in Eco-Conscious Narratives
raw_abstract: This article investigates how digital influencers in Portugal communicate sustainability on Instagram and how such messaging is interpreted by Generation Z audiences. While influencer culture plays a growing role in shaping sustainable consumption, little is known about the symbolic, aesthetic and emotional strategies through which sustainability is mediated – and even less about how these strategies are decoded by Gen Z publics. Addressing this gap, the study adopts a two-stage qualitative research design. Study 1 applies a Visual-Verbal Video Analysis (VVVA) to 200 Instagram Reels across four influencer tiers (mega, macro, micro and nano), examining how emotional coherence, aesthetic labor and symbolic distinction shape sustainability communication. Study 2 conducts focus group discussions with 29 Portuguese Gen Z users – using prompts on sustainable consumption, authenticity and emotional engagement – to explore how participants interpret emotional cues, assess authenticity and negotiate trust. Findings reveal a stratified symbolic economy of sustainable influence. Mega- and macro-influencers prioritize branded aesthetics and aspirational tone, often displacing sustainability into lifestyle tropes. In contrast, micro- and nano-influencers embed ecological values through visual minimalism, emotional intimacy and politicized storytelling. Gen Z participants exhibit ambivalent trust toward influencer messaging, relying on affective resonance and stylistic coherence rather than explicit claims. Performances of sincerity, marked by aesthetic simplicity, emotional modulation and symbolic restraint, emerge as key indicators of perceived credibility. The study concludes that sustainability on Instagram operates less as an informational discourse and more as an affective performance. Influencers function as cultural intermediaries who aestheticize ethical living through emotionally legible scripts, shaping how sustainability is visualized, interpreted and practiced within contemporary digital consumer culture.
title_en: Shaping Sustainable Communication on Instagram: How Digital Influencers in Portugal Engage Consumers in Eco-Conscious Narratives
title_zh: 在Instagram上塑造可持续传播：葡萄牙数字网红如何引导消费者参与生态意识叙事
abstract_en: This article investigates how digital influencers in Portugal communicate sustainability on Instagram and how such messaging is interpreted by Generation Z audiences. While influencer culture plays a growing role in shaping sustainable consumption, little is known about the symbolic, aesthetic and emotional strategies through which sustainability is mediated – and even less about how these strategies are decoded by Gen Z publics. Addressing this gap, the study adopts a two-stage qualitative research design. Study 1 applies a Visual-Verbal Video Analysis (VVVA) to 200 Instagram Reels across four influencer tiers (mega, macro, micro and nano), examining how emotional coherence, aesthetic labor and symbolic distinction shape sustainability communication. Study 2 conducts focus group discussions with 29 Portuguese Gen Z users – using prompts on sustainable consumption, authenticity and emotional engagement – to explore how participants interpret emotional cues, assess authenticity and negotiate trust. Findings reveal a stratified symbolic economy of sustainable influence. Mega- and macro-influencers prioritize branded aesthetics and aspirational tone, often displacing sustainability into lifestyle tropes. In contrast, micro- and nano-influencers embed ecological values through visual minimalism, emotional intimacy and politicized storytelling. Gen Z participants exhibit ambivalent trust toward influencer messaging, relying on affective resonance and stylistic coherence rather than explicit claims. Performances of sincerity, marked by aesthetic simplicity, emotional modulation and symbolic restraint, emerge as key indicators of perceived credibility. The study concludes that sustainability on Instagram operates less as an informational discourse and more as an affective performance. Influencers function as cultural intermediaries who aestheticize ethical living through emotionally legible scripts, shaping how sustainability is visualized, interpreted and practiced within contemporary digital consumer culture.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了葡萄牙数字网红如何在Instagram上传播可持续性，以及Z世代受众如何解读这些信息。尽管网红文化在塑造可持续消费方面发挥着越来越重要的作用，但人们对可持续性传播所采用的象征性、审美和情感策略知之甚少，更不用说Z世代公众如何解码这些策略了。为弥补这一空白，本研究采用了两阶段定性研究设计。研究1对四个网红层级（超级、宏观、微观和纳米）的200个Instagram Reels进行了视觉-言语视频分析（VVVA），考察情感连贯性、审美劳动和象征性区分如何塑造可持续性传播。研究2与29名葡萄牙Z世代用户进行了焦点小组讨论——使用关于可持续消费、真实性和情感参与的提示——探讨参与者如何解读情感线索、评估真实性和协商信任。研究结果揭示了可持续影响力的分层象征经济。超级和宏观网红优先考虑品牌美学和励志基调，往往将可持续性置换为生活方式比喻。相比之下，微观和纳米网红通过视觉极简主义、情感亲密性和政治化叙事嵌入生态价值观。Z世代参与者对网红信息表现出矛盾的信任，依赖情感共鸣和风格连贯性而非明确主张。真诚的表现——以审美简洁、情感调节和象征克制为特征——成为感知可信度的关键指标。研究得出结论，Instagram上的可持续性运作更像是一种情感表演，而非信息话语。网红作为文化中介，通过情感上可读的脚本将伦理生活审美化，塑造了可持续性在当代数字消费文化中的可视化、解读和实践方式。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了葡萄牙的网红如何在Instagram上谈论环保和可持续生活，以及年轻人（Z世代）如何看待这些内容。研究者分析了200个短视频，并与29名年轻人进行了小组讨论。他们发现，大网红更注重品牌和好看画面，而小网红则用更简单、更真诚的方式谈论环保。年轻人更相信那些看起来真实、不夸张的网红，而不是那些说得天花乱坠的。总的来说，Instagram上的环保内容更像是一种情感表演，而不是单纯的信息传递。
plain_summary_en: This study looks at how influencers in Portugal talk about sustainability on Instagram and how young people (Generation Z) perceive these messages. Researchers analyzed 200 short videos and held group discussions with 29 young people. They found that big influencers focus more on brand aesthetics and aspirational content, while smaller influencers use simpler, more genuine ways to talk about eco-friendly topics. Young people tend to trust influencers who seem authentic and not over-the-top. Overall, sustainability on Instagram is more about emotional performance than just sharing information.
research_direction_zh: 社交媒体上的可持续传播与消费者参与
research_direction_en: Sustainable communication and consumer engagement on social media
